key_insight: Geometry-aware operator learning can predict industrial crash dynamics with high fidelity, capturing complex deformation and transient responses more efficiently than traditional FEM.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster crashworthiness evaluation and design iteration for safety-critical automotive structures without sacrificing key physical accuracy.
method: GeoTransolver learns crash dynamics with geometry-aware cross-attention.
Compared one-shot, time-conditional, and autoregressive temporal rollout strategies.
Added FLARE low-rank attention to cut memory use and improve long-range transient prediction.
evidence_signal: Validated on complex bumper beam and full-vehicle crash datasets, with one-shot prediction reported as state-of-the-art in accuracy while reducing training overhead and inference latency.
watch_item: Performance depends on robust handling of contact nonlinearities and evolving deformation, so generalization to unseen crash scenarios should be checked carefully.
paper_title: High-Fidelity Industrial Crash Dynamics Prediction via Geometry-Aware Operator Learning with Memory-Efficient Low-Rank Attention
